data.information:
An evacuation shelter is available at the Pasadena Convention Center Exhibition Hall, 300 East Green Street, Pasadena, CA 91101 per the LA County website.

Parking is available at the parking structure across the street from the Convention Center, located at 155 E. Green St. Please enter from Marengo Ave.

Parking is also available at the Paseo parking structure, located at 360 E. Colorado Blvd. You are able to enter the structure from Colorado Blvd. or Green St.

The Humane Society is on-site to accept pets for boarding. Service animals will be allowed inside the Convention Center.

Residents are able to call 211 for information regarding shelters, and social and health services.

child_geo_events.address: 21900 Pacific Coast Hwy, Huntington Beach, CA 92646, USA
child_geo_events.name: Wildlife Assistance - Wetlands & Wildlife Care Center

data.information:
Update from Wetlands & Wildlife Care Center on Facebook:

With multiple large wildfires ongoing in Southern California, we wanted to provide resources to anyone who may come across injured wildlife. Several licensed wildlife rehabilitators in the area had to evacuate. One of our cherished centers, the California Wildlife Center in Calabasas, was one of them. They successfully evacuated all their patients. Their hotline is still being manned and may be able to offer you help with any wildlife (310) 458-9453

Please use caution if you come across injured wildlife. They are frightened and running for their lives. They have lost their homes, food sources, and shelter. If you live on the outskirts of the fire area, you may see many displaced wildlife. You can help them by leaving out bowls of water so they can rehydrate. Please do not leave out any food and be sure your pets are secure inside your home. If it is safe for you, place injured wildlife into an animal crate and transport it to a licensed wildlife rehabilitation center. The Pasadena Human Society is still helping injured wildlife in the Alta Dena area. You can reach them by calling (626) 344-1129.

If you are in an affected area and come across injured wildlife needing assistance, there are several nearby facilities located outside of the fire area. They have space available to help intake animals.

Please see the locations and contacts for these facilities should you need them:

Wetlands and Wildlife Care Center
21900 Pacific Coast Highway Huntington Beach, CA 92646
(714) 374-5587

Link to their post

child_geo_events.address: 361 S Raymond Ave, Pasadena, CA 91105, USA
child_geo_events.name: Small Animal Shelter - Pasadena Humane Society

data.information:
A small animal shelter is established at the Pasadena Humane Society for emergency sheltering. The shelter is located at 361 S Raymond Ave, Pasadena, CA 91105. The shelter states, "If you are being evacuated and can go somewhere with your pets–such as a friend or family member’s home–that is ideal. If you are evacuating, please take your animals with you. We are very thankful that the Pasadena Civic Center has allowed people to stay with their pets. We are helping in any way that we can–providing them with resources such as food and leashes for the displaced families. We also have pet food available at the shelter for those who need it. If you cannot go somewhere that allows your pet to stay with you, you can bring your animals to the shelter where we are offering emergency boarding.

Pets: If you have been impacted by the Eaton Fire and need help with a pet you’ve lost, found or had to leave behind in the fire zone, please contact our Dispatch at 626-792-7151 x 970.

Wildlife: If you need help with injured wildlife, please text or call 626-344-1129." per  Pasadena Humane

More details are located at this link.

With multiple large wildfires ongoing in Southern California, we wanted to provide resources to anyone who may come across injured wildlife. Several licensed wildlife rehabilitators in the area had to evacuate. One of our cherished centers, the California Wildlife Center in Calabasas, was one of them. Please use caution if you come across injured wildlife. They are frightened and running for their lives. They have lost their homes, food sources, and shelter. If you live on the outskirts of the fire area, you may see many displaced wildlife. You can help them by leaving out bowls of water so they can rehydrate. Please do not leave out any food and be sure your pets are secure inside your home. If it is safe for you, place injured wildlife into an animal crate and transport it to a licensed wildlife rehabilitation center. The Pasadena Human Society is still helping injured wildlife in the Alta Dena area. You can reach them by calling (626) 344-1129.

If you are in an affected area and come across injured wildlife needing assistance, there are several nearby facilities located outside of the fire area. They have space available to help intake animals.

21900 Pacific Coast Highway Huntington Beach, CA 92646
